heading: Prohibition on cooperation with the International Criminal Court
label: 7423 Prohibition on cooperation with the International Criminal Court
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (h). designated existing provisions as par. (1), inserted heading, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 117–328

Rule of Construction, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–328, div. K, title VII, § 7073(c)136 Stat. 5092Dec. 29, 2022“Nothing in this section [amending this section and ] may be construed to modify the existing roles or authorities of any Federal agency or official.”section 7433 of this title

children.text: apply only to cooperation with the International Criminal Court and shall not apply to cooperation with an ad hoc international criminal tribunal established by the United Nations Security Council before or after , to investigate and prosecute war crimes committed in a specific country or during a specific conflict; andAugust 2, 2002

children.label: A
children.text: any action permitted under ; orsection 7427 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: communication by the United States of its policy with respect to a matter.

children.heading: Prohibition on responding to requests for cooperation
children.label: b Prohibition on responding to requests for cooperation
children.text: Notwithstanding or any other provision of law, no United States Court, and no agency or entity of any State or local government, including any court, may cooperate with the International Criminal Court in response to a request for cooperation submitted by the International Criminal Court pursuant to the Rome Statute.section 1782 of title 28

children.heading: Prohibition on transmittal of letters rogatory from the International Criminal Court
children.label: c Prohibition on transmittal of letters rogatory from the International Criminal Court
children.text: Notwithstanding or any other provision of law, no agency of the United States Government may transmit for execution any letter rogatory issued, or other request for cooperation made, by the International Criminal Court to the tribunal, officer, or agency in the United States to whom it is addressed.section 1781 of title 28

children.heading: Prohibition on extradition to the International Criminal Court
children.label: d Prohibition on extradition to the International Criminal Court
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no agency or entity of the United States Government or of any State or local government may extradite any person from the United States to the International Criminal Court, nor support the transfer of any United States citizen or permanent resident alien to the International Criminal Court.

children.heading: Prohibition on provision of support to the International Criminal Court
children.label: e Prohibition on provision of support to the International Criminal Court
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no agency or entity of the United States Government or of any State or local government, including any court, may provide support to the International Criminal Court.

children.heading: Prohibition on use of appropriated funds to assist the International Criminal Court
children.label: f Prohibition on use of appropriated funds to assist the International Criminal Court
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no funds appropriated under any provision of law may be used for the purpose of assisting the investigation, arrest, detention, extradition, or prosecution of any United States citizen or permanent resident alien by the International Criminal Court.

children.heading: Restriction on assistance pursuant to mutual legal assistance treaties
children.label: g Restriction on assistance pursuant to mutual legal assistance treaties
children.text: The United States shall exercise its rights to limit the use of assistance provided under all treaties and executive agreements for mutual legal assistance in criminal matters, multilateral conventions with legal assistance provisions, and extradition treaties, to which the United States is a party, and in connection with the execution or issuance of any letter rogatory, to prevent the transfer to, or other use by, the International Criminal Court of any assistance provided by the United States under such treaties and letters rogatory.

children.heading: Prohibition on investigative activities of agents
children.label: h Prohibition on investigative activities of agents

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No agent of the International Criminal Court may conduct, in the United States or any territory subject to the jurisdiction of the United States, any investigative activity relating to a preliminary inquiry, investigation, prosecution, or other proceeding at the International Criminal Court.

children.label: A
children.text: relate solely to investigations and prosecutions of foreign persons for crimes within the jurisdiction of the International Criminal Court related to the Situation in Ukraine; and

children.label: B
children.text: are undertaken in concurrence with the Attorney General.
